title: Execution order for public-release finish tasks
recommendation: All planned finish tasks are complete. Publish only with the current bounded claim posture intact.

tasks.why_first: This reduced wording drift before visual polish, so later edits stayed smaller and more stable.
scope: headlines, key claims, caution language, and repeated terminology
tasks.output: one aligned EN/DE canonical set with fewer wording mismatches
done_when: same core claims appear in EN and DE
same caution posture appears in EN and DE
no obvious terminology drift in canonical pages

tasks.task: Release-grade polish pass on site + README
tasks.why_second: Once wording was stable, presentation polish could be applied without being invalidated immediately afterward.
scope: landing page spacing, ordering, and emphasis
tasks.output: cleaner first impression and more release-like presentation
done_when: README scans cleanly top to bottom
no section feels obviously drafty or redundant

tasks.why_third: After wording and presentation settled, the bounded proof case could stay visible without becoming the main claim.
tasks.output: explicit editorial stance on how prominent the bounded proof case should be
done_when: proof-case prominence is an intentional choice, not a residue of build history
the chosen prominence is consistent across README, index, and site

tasks.task: Final manual release review + confirmatory go/no-go check
tasks.why_last: This confirmed the final publication posture after content, polish, and editorial framing were stable enough to judge.
tasks.output: final release posture: publish now, if desired
done_when: a release decision can be stated clearly with reasons
remaining gaps are explicitly accepted as optional refinements
public-facing claim wording is signed off for a bounded release

stop_rule: Publish only if the current bounded claim posture and privacy boundary are kept intact.
